verificationStatus: not-verified-against-gazette — this risk register contains constructed-indicative risks illustrative of typical NCII operator concerns under Act 854; specific risk ratings, controls, and treatment plans are examples and must be tailored to each entity's context
title: NCII Cyber Security Risk Register
description: Indicative risk register for Malaysian NCII operators under the Cyber Security Act 2024 (Act 854). Contains 20 risks across 7 categories, assessed using the 5x5 likelihood-impact matrix.

risks.title: APT Targeting NCII Systems
risks.description: Advanced persistent threat (APT) groups, including nation-state actors, conduct sustained intrusion campaigns against Malaysian NCII systems to exfiltrate sensitive data, establish persistent access, or pre-position for future disruptive operations.
existingControls: Network segmentation between NCII and corporate networks
Endpoint detection and response (EDR) on critical servers
Threat intelligence feeds from NACSA and sector ISAC
risks.treatmentPlan: Deploy advanced threat hunting capability; implement zero-trust network architecture for NCII zones; participate in NACSA threat intelligence sharing program under s25; conduct annual red team exercise simulating APT scenarios.

risks.title: Ransomware on Critical NCII Systems
risks.description: Ransomware operators encrypt or destroy NCII system data and backups, causing prolonged service disruption to essential services. Double extortion tactics may result in exfiltration of sensitive NCII operational data before encryption.
existingControls: Offline backup strategy with regular testing
Email gateway with advanced malware detection
Network segmentation limiting lateral movement
risks.treatmentPlan: Transfer residual financial impact through comprehensive cyber insurance policy covering ransomware incidents, business interruption, data recovery costs, and regulatory fines. Policy must explicitly cover NCII systems and Act 854 notification obligations. Retain technical mitigation controls (immutable backups, application whitelisting) as insurance prerequisite. Insurance broker to confirm coverage for 6-hour incident notification penalties. Annual policy review aligned with risk reassessment cycle.

risks.title: Supply Chain Compromise of NCII Vendor
risks.description: A trusted vendor, managed service provider, or software supplier with privileged access to NCII systems is compromised, providing adversaries with an indirect pathway into the entity's critical infrastructure.
existingControls: Vendor security assessment questionnaire for critical suppliers
Contractual security requirements for NCII-touching vendors
Privileged access management for third-party connections
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement continuous vendor risk monitoring for Tier 1 NCII suppliers; require SOC 2 Type II or equivalent attestation; deploy separate jump servers for vendor access with full session recording; include supply chain scenarios in incident response exercises.

risks.title: Insider Sabotage of NCII Systems
risks.description: A malicious insider with privileged access deliberately disrupts, damages, or exfiltrates data from NCII systems, motivated by financial gain, coercion, ideological reasons, or grievance.
existingControls: Background checks for personnel with NCII system access
Privileged access management with approval workflows
User behavior analytics on critical system accounts
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement separation of duties for critical NCII operations; deploy data loss prevention (DLP) for NCII data repositories; establish insider threat program with HR, Legal, and Security participation; periodic re-vetting of personnel with elevated access.

risks.title: NACSA Reporting Failure (Section 23 Non-Compliance)
risks.description: The entity fails to submit required risk assessment or audit reports to the Chief Executive and sector lead within the mandated timeframe, or submits reports that are materially incomplete or inaccurate.
existingControls: Regulatory compliance calendar with automated reminders
Dedicated NCII Compliance Officer role
Pre-submission quality review process
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement compliance management system tracking all NACSA submission deadlines; establish 30-day pre-submission internal review process; maintain standing engagement with CE-approved assessor/auditor; conduct annual compliance readiness self-assessment.

risks.title: Unpatched NCII Systems
risks.description: Critical and high vulnerabilities in NCII systems remain unpatched beyond acceptable timeframes due to operational constraints, compatibility concerns, or inadequate patch management processes, providing known exploitation paths for attackers.
existingControls: Monthly vulnerability scanning of NCII assets
Patch management policy with SLA by severity
Change advisory board review for critical patches
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement automated patch deployment for non-disruptive patches; establish emergency patching procedure bypassing CAB for CVSS 9.0+ vulnerabilities on NCII systems; deploy virtual patching (WAF/IPS rules) as interim compensating control; track patch compliance as board-level KPI.

risks.title: Unauthorized Remote Access to NCII Systems
risks.description: Attackers or unauthorized personnel gain remote access to NCII systems through compromised VPN credentials, exposed management interfaces, misconfigured remote desktop services, or exploitation of remote access tools.
existingControls: Multi-factor authentication on all remote access pathways
VPN with certificate-based authentication for NCII access
Network access control restricting remote access to approved endpoints
risks.treatmentPlan: Eliminate remote access to NCII systems entirely. All NCII administration and operations to be performed on-site from physically secured facilities only. Disable all VPN, RDP, and remote management interfaces on NCII network segments. Vendor maintenance requiring system access must be conducted on-premises with escort. Emergency out-of-hours access provided via on-call personnel physically attending the NCII facility. This eliminates the remote access attack vector at the cost of operational flexibility.

risks.title: NCII Asset Inventory Gaps
risks.description: The entity's NCII asset register is incomplete, outdated, or inaccurate, resulting in unmanaged assets within the NCII perimeter that are not subject to security controls, monitoring, or risk assessment.
existingControls: NCII asset register maintained in CMDB
Quarterly asset discovery scans
Change management process requiring NCII impact assessment
risks.treatmentPlan: Deploy continuous asset discovery tools covering IT, OT, and cloud environments; implement automated CMDB reconciliation; require NCII impact assessment as mandatory step in change management; conduct annual physical audit of NCII facilities.
risks.owner: IT Asset Manager / NCII Compliance Officer

risks.title: Incident Response Delay Beyond 6-Hour Window
risks.description: The entity fails to detect, triage, and report a significant cyber security incident to the Chief Executive within the mandatory 6-hour notification window under s26, triggering regulatory non-compliance and potential enforcement action.
existingControls: 24/7 security operations center (SOC) monitoring NCII systems
Incident response plan with s26 notification procedure
Pre-drafted NACSA notification templates
risks.treatmentPlan: Conduct quarterly incident response tabletop exercises including s26 notification drill; implement automated incident classification that flags potential s26-triggering events; establish direct communication channel with NACSA duty officer; maintain on-call roster for incident commander and NCII Compliance Officer.

risks.title: Cross-Sector Cascade Failure
risks.description: A cyber security incident in one NCII sector cascades to other sectors through interdependencies (e.g., energy disruption affecting banking and healthcare IT), causing multi-sector essential service failures.
existingControls: Cross-sector dependency mapping in risk assessment
Business continuity plan addressing upstream dependencies
Participation in sector-level information sharing under s25
risks.treatmentPlan: Map all cross-sector NCII dependencies and single points of failure; develop sector-specific continuity plans for upstream service disruption; participate in NACSA-led cross-sector exercises; establish mutual aid agreements with peer NCII entities in dependent sectors.

risks.title: Third-Party Managed Service Compromise
risks.description: A managed security service provider (MSSP), cloud provider, or outsourced IT provider hosting or managing NCII systems is breached, exposing the entity's NCII data or systems through the provider's compromised infrastructure.
existingControls: Contractual incident notification obligations for managed service providers
Annual security audit right in service agreements
Data encryption for NCII data at rest and in transit with provider
risks.treatmentPlan: Require managed service providers to hold valid Part V licence under Act 854; implement continuous security monitoring of provider connections; establish provider incident response coordination procedure; maintain capability to insource critical functions within 90 days of provider failure.

risks.title: Social Engineering of NCII Personnel
risks.description: Threat actors use spear phishing, voice phishing (vishing), SMS phishing (smishing), or social media manipulation to compromise credentials or induce NCII personnel to perform unauthorized actions.
existingControls: Security awareness training program with phishing simulations
Email security gateway with anti-phishing capabilities
MFA on all NCII system accounts reducing credential-only compromise impact
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement targeted social engineering training for NCII personnel; deploy FIDO2/hardware token authentication for privileged NCII accounts; establish reporting channel for suspicious communications; conduct quarterly spear phishing simulations targeting NCII-access staff.

risks.title: Physical Security Breach at NCII Facilities
risks.description: Unauthorized physical access to NCII data centers, control rooms, or network facilities enables direct system compromise, hardware tampering, or theft of storage media containing NCII data.
existingControls: Multi-layer physical access controls (card, biometric, mantrap)
CCTV surveillance with 90-day retention at NCII facilities
Visitor management and escort policy for restricted areas
risks.treatmentPlan: Residual risk of 4 (Low band) is within organizational risk appetite given existing multi-layer physical controls (card, biometric, mantrap, CCTV, visitor management). Existing controls reduce likelihood to 1 (Rare). Cost of further mitigation (e.g., armed guards, Faraday caging) exceeds the expected loss. Risk accepted by CISO with annual review. Acceptance documented in board risk report.

risks.title: Data Exfiltration from NCII Systems
risks.description: Sensitive data is exfiltrated from NCII systems through covert channels (DNS tunneling, steganography, encrypted tunnels to external hosts) or overt channels (unauthorized cloud storage, removable media, email to personal accounts).
existingControls: Data loss prevention (DLP) monitoring on NCII network egress
USB and removable media restrictions on NCII endpoints
Network flow analysis detecting anomalous outbound data volumes
risks.treatmentPlan: Deploy advanced DLP with content-aware inspection for NCII-classified data; implement DNS inspection to detect tunneling; restrict cloud storage access from NCII network zones; establish data classification scheme aligned with NCII asset criticality levels.

risks.title: NCII System Misconfiguration
risks.description: Security misconfiguration of NCII systems (default credentials, unnecessary services, overly permissive firewall rules, insecure cloud settings) creates exploitable weaknesses that bypass existing security controls.
existingControls: Security baseline configurations for major NCII platforms
Configuration scanning in vulnerability management cycle
Change management process with security review
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement infrastructure-as-code with security guardrails for NCII systems; deploy configuration compliance monitoring with automated drift detection; establish CIS Benchmark compliance targets for all NCII platforms; require security configuration review as part of change approval.

risks.title: Inadequate Access Controls on NCII Systems
risks.description: Excessive access permissions, stale accounts, shared credentials, or inadequate role-based access controls on NCII systems expand the attack surface and enable privilege escalation or unauthorized data access.
existingControls: Role-based access control (RBAC) framework for NCII systems
Quarterly access review of privileged accounts
Automated deprovisioning linked to HR termination process
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement identity governance platform with automated access certification; deploy privileged access management (PAM) with session recording for NCII systems; establish least-privilege principle enforcement with exception tracking; integrate identity analytics for anomalous access pattern detection.

risks.title: Failure to Conduct Mandatory Risk Assessment
risks.description: The entity fails to conduct the mandatory periodic risk assessment by a CE-approved assessor within the required timeframe, violating s21 and P.U.(A) 219/2024, exposing the entity to enforcement action and leaving risks unidentified.
existingControls: Regulatory compliance calendar with CEO-level visibility
Retainer agreement with CE-approved assessor
Annual budget allocation for risk assessment engagement
risks.treatmentPlan: Establish 6-month pre-assessment planning cycle; maintain relationships with multiple CE-approved assessors as backup; include risk assessment budget in mandatory NCII compliance spending; implement compliance tracking dashboard with board reporting.

risks.title: Non-Compliance with NACSA Directives
risks.description: The entity fails to comply with binding Chief Executive directives issued under s15, whether through ignorance of newly issued directives, misinterpretation of requirements, or failure to implement mandated measures within specified timeframes.
existingControls: NACSA regulatory monitoring and alert process
Legal counsel review of new directives within 7 days of issuance
Compliance gap analysis for each new directive
risks.treatmentPlan: Subscribe to NACSA Cyber Security Portal notifications; establish cross-functional directive implementation task force; maintain directive compliance matrix with status tracking; conduct quarterly compliance self-assessment against all active directives.

risks.title: IoT/OT Convergence Risk in NCII Systems
risks.description: Convergence of operational technology (OT), Internet of Things (IoT), and IT systems within the NCII perimeter introduces legacy devices with limited security capabilities, creating pathways for attackers to move between IT and OT environments.
existingControls: IT/OT network segmentation with industrial DMZ
OT asset inventory maintained separately from IT CMDB
OT-specific monitoring and anomaly detection
risks.treatmentPlan: Deploy OT-aware network monitoring (e.g., Claroty, Nozomi Networks); implement unidirectional security gateways for critical IT-OT interfaces; develop OT-specific incident response procedures; establish IoT device lifecycle management including firmware update processes; conduct OT-focused penetration testing annually.

risks.title: Cryptographic Key Compromise
risks.description: Cryptographic keys used to protect NCII data (encryption keys, TLS certificates, code signing keys, HSM master keys) are compromised through theft, insecure storage, weak key management practices, or cryptographic algorithm vulnerabilities.
existingControls: Hardware security modules (HSM) for critical key storage
Certificate lifecycle management with automated renewal
Key rotation policy with annual rotation for critical keys
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement enterprise key management system with FIPS 140-2 Level 3 HSMs; establish crypto-agility program for post-quantum readiness; deploy certificate transparency monitoring; develop key compromise response procedure with pre-staged replacement keys; conduct annual key management audit.
